title: Managed audit manual sandbox connection credential resolver endpoint handle allowlist approval contract intake

endpointHandleAllowlistApprovalContract.sourceSpan: Node v319 closure review + Node v313 catalog
endpointHandleAllowlistApprovalContract.purpose: Define the non-secret endpoint handle allowlist approval shape required before any later resolver can discuss a managed audit sandbox endpoint.

requiredFields.label: Endpoint handle
requiredFields.purpose: Identify which endpoint handle was reviewed without exposing the raw endpoint URL.

requiredFields.label: Approval correlation id
requiredFields.purpose: Bind the endpoint allowlist review to the signed approval chain.

requiredFields.label: Operator identity handle
requiredFields.acceptedShape: operator identity handle, no credential value
requiredFields.purpose: Identify the requesting operator without embedding secret material.

requiredFields.label: Reviewer identity handle
requiredFields.acceptedShape: reviewer identity handle, no private key
requiredFields.purpose: Identify the human reviewer without carrying credentials or signing keys.

requiredFields.label: Policy version
requiredFields.purpose: Bind the endpoint allowlist approval to a known review policy contract.

requiredFields.label: Approval status
requiredFields.purpose: Keep this version contract-only and status-based.

requiredFields.label: Issued at
requiredFields.purpose: Declare when the endpoint handle allowlist approval was issued.

requiredFields.label: Expires at
requiredFields.purpose: Prevent stale endpoint allowlist approvals from being treated as current.

requiredFields.label: Revocation marker
requiredFields.acceptedShape: boolean marker plus optional evidence handle
requiredFields.purpose: Make revocation explicit without reading any secret provider state.

requiredFields.label: Audit digest
requiredFields.acceptedShape: sha256 digest or equivalent stable digest
requiredFields.purpose: Prove contract immutability without embedding raw credential or endpoint material.

prohibitedFields.reason: Credential values must not enter the endpoint allowlist approval contract.
Raw endpoint URLs must not enter this contract; only endpoint handles and allowlist review status are allowed.
Provider configuration would turn this contract into implementation.
Resolver client configuration is not allowed in a contract-only intake.
Runtime bindings for providers or clients remain out of scope.
No HTTP/TCP payload may be prepared or sent by v320.
Approval ledger writes remain outside this Node contract.
Schema migration SQL is prohibited in this intake.

rejectionReasons.message: The endpoint handle allowlist approval contract fields are missing.
Credential values are not allowed; only handles and review statuses are allowed.
Raw endpoint URLs are not allowed; this intake accepts only endpoint handle and allowlist review metadata.
Secret provider and resolver client config are prohibited in this intake.
Ledger writes, schema migration, deployment, and rollback execution are prohibited.

noGoBoundaries.message: v320 must not read managed audit credential values.
v320 must not parse or render raw endpoint URLs.
v320 must not instantiate secret providers.
v320 must not instantiate resolver clients.
v320 must not send HTTP/TCP requests.
v320 must not write approval ledger or schema state.
v320 must not automatically start Java, mini-kv, or external audit services.
v320 must not implement a runtime shell.
v320 must not invoke a runtime shell.

upstreamEchoRequests.requestedEcho: Read-only echo of the Node v320 endpoint-handle allowlist approval contract.
Non-participation receipt proving mini-kv does not store raw endpoint URLs, validate allowlist authority, or become authority for endpoint handles.

necessityProof.blockerResolved: v319 completed the credential-handle-approval prerequisite and named endpoint-handle-allowlist-approval as the next concrete missing contract.
necessityProof.consumer: Java v147 + mini-kv v140, then Node v321
necessityProof.whyV319CannotBeReused: v319 is a closure review only; it proves the credential-handle-approval prerequisite is complete but does not define endpoint handle allowlist approval fields for upstream echo.
necessityProof.existingReportReuseDecision: Reuse v319 as source state and v313 as the prerequisite catalog; create v320 only for the endpoint-handle allowlist approval contract intake.
necessityProof.stopCondition: Stop if the contract requires credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client configuration, external requests, runtime shell implementation or invocation, ledger/schema writes, mini-kv authority, or automatic upstream start.

warnings.message: v320 defines an endpoint-handle allowlist approval contract only; no-network fixture and abort/rollback semantics remain separate prerequisites.
An endpoint handle allowlist approval status does not authorize Node to parse raw endpoint URLs or send HTTP/TCP requests.

recommendations.message: After v320 is archived, Java v147 and mini-kv v140 can run in parallel as read-only echo and non-participation receipt work.
Keep the endpoint handle allowlist approval as handles, statuses, digest, and timestamps only; never add credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client config, or HTTP/TCP payloads.

nextActions: Archive Node v320 as the endpoint-handle allowlist approval contract-only intake.
After v320 is complete, Java v147 and mini-kv v140 can run in parallel as read-only echo and non-participation receipt work.
Node v321 must wait for both Java v147 and mini-kv v140 before verifying endpoint-handle allowlist approval echo alignment.

Stop immediately if the endpoint-handle allowlist contract tries to include credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client config, external request payloads, ledger/schema writes, runtime shell behavior, or automatic upstream start.
